mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration that triggers immediate equity risk-off positioning and negative short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns remain positive in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.4pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength that sustains corporate earnings growth and drives positive quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce positive 60-day forward returns in at least 60% of observed cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during periods of low inflation signals robust supply-side capacity utilization that boosts equity valuations and generates positive monthly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO momentum fails to predict positive 20-day returns when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 0.5%, or the signal loses predictive power in high-inflation regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price index readings force markets to price in tighter monetary policy, compressing equity multiples and producing negative short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in over 50% of months where CPIAUCSL rises above 0.3%, or the signal shows zero predictive accuracy in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Strong nonfarm payroll growth combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms labor market tightening that supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE yields negative 60-day forward returns in more than 45% of cases, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly real GDP contraction triggers recession fears that immediately depress equity risk premiums and produce negative monthly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns remain positive in at least 55% of quarters following a negative GDPC1 reading, or the threshold signal fails to separate bull and bear outcomes.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle signals demand destruction that accelerates equity sell-offs and generates negative short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO momentum during tightening periods fails to predict negative 5-day returns, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below random chance in walk-forward validation.
